You are a sub-agent performing a cross-service refactor.

## Task
Extract the shared `RateLimiter` struct from `svc-auth` and `svc-orders` into
a new shared library `lib/rate-limiter`.  Both services must import the library
after the refactor.

## Context
This is a Large-complexity task: it touches 3+ files across 2 services.
Full SDD governance is required.

Governance rules you must follow:
- [`blast-radius`] — assess impact before making changes across services
- [`scope-proportionality`] — keep the refactor scoped to the rate limiter only
- [`decomposition`] — break into sub-tasks: extract → update svc-auth → update svc-orders → test
- [`impact-analysis`] — run impact analysis before applying changes
- [`adversarial-review`] — perform an adversarial review of the design

Also active:
- [`acceptance-criteria`] — enumerate all 47 call-sites before starting
- [`agent-quality`] — report actual counts, not "all occurrences"
- [`definition-of-done`] — Large DoD: readiness_check_pass + integration_tests + adversarial_review

## HALT Protocol
This task triggers HALT (multi-service change).  Output PLAN + SCOPE + RISK and
wait for approval before executing.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `grep -rl "RateLimiter" svc-auth/ svc-orders/ --include="*.go" | wc -l` = 0 (no local copies)
2. `grep -rl "lib/rate-limiter" svc-auth/ svc-orders/ --include="*.go" | wc -l` > 0
3. `go build ./...` exits 0
4. `go test ./...` exits 0
5. Coverage on `lib/rate-limiter` >= 80%

## Auto-Refine
Retry up to 3 times on build/test failure.  Escalate per [`agent-escalation`].
